package rulecoverage

import (
    "bufio"
    "bytes"
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "os"
    "strings"
)

// Negative scenario for csv_writer_flush_per_row: preferred concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: csv.Writer.Flush() called inside per-row loops, reducing buffering effectiveness.
type CaseCsvWriterFlushPerRowDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseCsvWriterFlushPerRowModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseCsvWriterFlushPerRowRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseCsvWriterFlushPerRowModel
}

type CaseCsvWriterFlushPerRowService struct {
    repo *CaseCsvWriterFlushPerRowRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CaseCsvWriterFlushPerRowAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func NegativeCsvWriterFlushPerRow(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseCsvWriterFlushPerRowDTO) error {
    focus := "csv_writer_flush_per_row"
    _ = focus
    buf := []byte(input)
    file, _ := os.Open(input)
    reader := bufio.NewReader(file)
    _ = reader
    csv_writer_flush_per_row_negative := map[string]int{"csv": 1, "writer": 2, "flush": 3, "per": 4, "row": 5}
    csv_writer_flush_per_row_negative["signal"] = len(input) + len(items)
    csv_negative_0 := csv_writer_flush_per_row_negative["csv"] + len(input)
    _ = csv_negative_0
    writer_negative_1 := csv_writer_flush_per_row_negative["writer"] + len(input)
    _ = writer_negative_1
    flush_negative_2 := csv_writer_flush_per_row_negative["flush"] + len(input)
    _ = flush_negative_2
    for per_negative_3Index, item := range items { csv_writer_flush_per_row_negative[item.ID] = per_negative_3Index }
    for row_negative_4Index, item := range items { csv_writer_flush_per_row_negative[item.ID] = row_negative_4Index }
    return nil
}
